title: nw digest · for lolo
html:
Friday, February 27, 2026 curated for lolo
the field right now
140,000+
patients treated with Spravato worldwide
$5.35B
U.S. ketamine clinic market (2025)
late 2026
Compass psilocybin FDA decision expected
clinical trials & outcomes
trial
The “Montreal Model” — ketamine + psychotherapy yields sustained depression relief
First-of-its-kind trial combining ketamine with psychotherapy and supportive environments. Depression scores dropped ~30%, and unlike standard ketamine where benefits fade in days, improvement lasted 8+ weeks. Suggests context matters as much as chemistry.
trial
KARMA-Dep 2 — serial ketamine infusions as adjunctive inpatient therapy
JAMA Psychiatry, Dec 2025. RCT evaluating adjunctive ketamine vs. midazolam for inpatient depression. One of the strongest designs testing ketamine in acute care — the setting where rapid onset matters most.
trial
Stanford: ketamine under anesthesia no better than placebo
The provocative one. 40 participants given ketamine or saline after being put under for surgery. Depression improved equally in both groups. Forces the question every clinician in this space lives with: pharmacology, psychology, or both?
trial
Extended-release oral ketamine tablets (R-107) — Phase 2 in Nature Medicine
Minimal dissociation, no BP changes, most dosing at home. If Phase 3 holds, this moves ketamine from clinic-bound infusions to something patients take in their own kitchen. The delivery model changes everything.
trial
Oral ketamine for MDD and bipolar — RCT and meta-analysis (592 patients)
Oct 2025. Side effects mild and transient. The accessibility and low cost of oral ketamine warrants further investigation across different populations and settings. The equity argument is building.
mechanism & science
mechanism
Towards an expanded neurocognitive account of ketamine’s rapid antidepressant effects
Int. J. Neuropsychopharmacology, Feb 2025
Goes beyond NMDAR→glutamate→BDNF→mTORC1. Argues we need a neurocognitive framework — not just molecular — to explain why ketamine works. Bridging synaptogenesis and subjective experience.
mechanism
Variations in BDNF and their role in the neurotrophic antidepressant mechanisms of ketamine vs. esketamine
Int. J. Molecular Sciences, Dec 2024
Key finding: conventional antidepressants upregulate BDNF expression but not release. Ketamine does both, rapidly. This may explain the speed difference and has implications for how we think about maintenance dosing.
mechanism
Rapid neuroplasticity changes and response to IV ketamine — RCT in treatment-resistant depression
Translational Psychiatry — 98 adults, 2:1 randomization
Quantified gray matter microstructural changes on a 24-hour timescale in humans. Moves the neuroplasticity story from rodent models into clinical evidence you can point to.
regulatory & pipeline
regulatory
Spravato approved as first standalone monotherapy for TRD
Jan 2025. No longer requires a concurrent oral antidepressant. 22.5% remission vs 7.6% placebo by week 4. Opens the door for patients with SSRI intolerance or polypharmacy concerns.
pipeline
Compass Pathways psilocybin — rolling FDA submission, decision expected late 2026
If approved, first psilocybin therapy on the market. Post-MDMA rejection, they’re framing it as drug therapy, not drug-assisted psychotherapy. The regulatory framing might matter as much as the Phase 3 data.
regulatory
DEA extends telemedicine ketamine prescribing through 2026
COVID-era remote prescribing rules extended one more year. The DEA is building a permanent framework. For anyone doing at-home protocols or working across state lines — the window is open but the clock is ticking.
pipeline
What the MDMA rejection means for the rest of psychedelic medicine
The Lykos CRL is reshaping every psychedelic company’s trial design. Key lesson: “drug-assisted psychotherapy” adds regulatory surface area. Future approvals will likely frame compounds as standalone drugs. This affects how ketamine-assisted therapy gets studied too.
Built this for someone working where suffering meets chemistry. The Stanford anesthesia study and the Montreal Model are the same question from opposite directions — is it the molecule or the moment? Probably both, and the field is finally designing trials sophisticated enough to tease that apart. Meanwhile the oral extended-release data might quietly matter more than any of it. If ketamine leaves the clinic and enters the home, the entire access equation changes — and that’s the gap that needs closing most.
— nw
